description: When the user opens the Shop (Products) page from the navigation, the page stays blank or shows an error instead of displaying the product list and shop content.
expectedOutcome: The Products page should render normally with the product grid, title, and the line showing theme, currency, and locale (e.g. 'Theme: ... · USD · en').
hint: Check where the Products page is rendered in the component tree relative to PreferencesProvider.
symptom: The Products (Shop) page is blank or crashes when you open it.
solution: Render the Products route inside PreferencesProvider so that the Products component (which consumes PreferencesContext via usePreferences) is a descendant of the provider. Move the /products route from the first Routes block into the Routes block that is wrapped by PreferencesProvider.
solutionCode: In App.jsx: remove the standalone Routes that only contains the /products route, and add <Route path="/products" element={<Products />} /> back inside the Routes that are wrapped by <PreferencesProvider>.
buggyCode:
<main className="main-content">
  <Routes>
    <Route path="/products" element={<Products />} />
  </Routes>
  <PreferencesProvider>
    <Routes>
      ... other routes without /products
    </Routes>
  </PreferencesProvider>
</main>
correctFix:
<main className="main-content">
  <PreferencesProvider>
    <Routes>
      <Route path="/products" element={<Products />} />
      ... all other routes
    </Routes>
  </PreferencesProvider>
</main>
playwrightTestSnippets: test('Products page renders with context (currency and locale)', async ({ page }) => { ... await expect(page.getByTestId('products-page')).toBeVisible(); ... await expect(page.getByText(/Theme:.*USD|currency|locale/i)).toBeVisible(); })
